q1_sink_dangerous.evidence: Template('Result: ' + fragment).render() on line 132 is an unsandboxed Jinja2 template instantiation with user-controlled source, which can achieve RCE via SSTI.

mock_guidance.target: Mock jinja2.Template (constructor or the instance's render method) to intercept the template source string passed to Template().
mock_guidance.capture: Capture the full template-source argument ('Result: ' + fragment) passed to the Template constructor. Assert that it does not contain Jinja2 syntax delimiters ({{, }}, {%, %}) that originate from user input (the fragment portion).
mock_guidance.fake_behavior: Return a mock Template instance whose render() method returns a safe placeholder string (e.g., 'MOCKED_TEMPLATE_OUTPUT') so the subsequent HttpResponse construction on the return line succeeds without executing the real template engine.
notes: Because default jinja2.Template is not sandboxed, calling render() with attacker-controlled source can execute arbitrary Python. Mocking is mandatory.
The check should verify that the fragment portion of the source string contains no template expressions.
